member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.BackgroundWorkers.AuditLogCleanupWorker
member.summary: Background worker that periodically cleans up old audit logs. Audit logs are important for compliance, but keeping them forever wastes storage. This worker archives or deletes logs older than a configured retention period (e.g., 90 days).

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.BackgroundWorkers.BackgroundServiceBase`1
member.summary: Abstract base class for background workers that provides common timer loop, error handling, and cancellation token checking functionality.
member.typeparam: The concrete worker type for logging purposes.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.BackgroundWorkers.BackgroundServiceBase`1.ExecuteAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Main execution loop that handles timer logic, cancellation, and error handling.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.BackgroundWorkers.BackgroundServiceBase`1.ExecuteCycleAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Executes a single cycle of the worker's main logic.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.BackgroundWorkers.BackgroundServiceBase`1.GetErrorDelay(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets the delay to use after an error occurs. Override this method to provide custom error delay behavior.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.BackgroundWorkers.BackgroundServiceBase`1.CreateScope
member.summary: Creates a new service scope for resolving scoped dependencies.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.BackgroundWorkers.KeyRotationScheduler
member.summary: Background worker that automatically rotates API keys nearing expiration. Runs on a daily cadence and delegates rotation logic to . The look-ahead window and replacement key TTL are configurable via appsettings under the KeyRotation section.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.BackgroundWorkers.KeyRotationScheduler.ApplyJitter(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Applies a random jitter to the provided interval based on the configured jitter percentage. When the jitter percentage is 0, the original interval is returned unchanged.
member.returns: Interval with jitter applied.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.BackgroundWorkers.RateLimitResetScheduler
member.summary: Background worker that manages rate limit window resets. Clears expired rate limit counters from cache at appropriate times. This ensures that users get fresh quotas when their time windows reset. Runs periodically to check and reset limits that have expired.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.BackgroundWorkers.UsageAggregationWorker
member.summary: Background worker that periodically aggregates usage data. Takes individual request records and groups them into hourly/daily summaries for efficient analytics queries. This reduces database load for reporting. Runs hourly by default (configurable).

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Caching.CacheKeyGenerator.ComputeParameterHash(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Creates a hash of parameters for consistent cache key generation. Order-independent so different parameter orders hash to same value. Uses ArrayPool for the char and byte encoding buffers to avoid creating an intermediate List<string>, a joined string, and a heap-allocated byte array on every external-API cache lookup.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Caching.ICacheProvider
member.summary: Interface for cache abstraction. Implementations can use different backends (in-memory, Redis, Memcached) without changing calling code. This is critical for supporting both single-instance and distributed deployments. Methods return ValueTask so that synchronous-completing implementations (e.g. InMemoryCacheProvider) avoid allocating a Task state-machine object per call.

member.summary: Retrieves a value from cache by key.

member.summary: Removes a value from cache.

member.summary: Checks if a key exists in cache.

member.summary: Atomically increments a counter. Used for rate limiting tracking.

member.summary: Removes all entries matching a pattern (use cautiously).

member.summary: Extension methods for registering request coalescing services into the dependency injection container.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.RequestCoalescingExtensions.AddRequestCoalescing(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Adds to the service collection as a singleton.
member.remarks:
The service must be registered as a singleton because it maintains a shared dictionary of in-flight requests that spans all HTTP scopes. Registering it as scoped or transient would prevent concurrent requests from finding each other's in-flight tasks and defeat the purpose of coalescing.
Call this method inside your service registration setup, for example:
builder.Services.AddRequestCoalescing();
Then inject wherever expensive operations such as database lookups or external API calls should be deduplicated under concurrent load.
member.param: The to add the service to.
member.returns: The same to allow method chaining.

member.name: P:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.GatewayConfiguration.FailOpenOnKeyStoreUnavailable
member.summary: When true the gateway allows requests through unauthenticated if the key store is unreachable (fail-open). When false (default) a 503 is returned so clients know to retry once the store recovers (fail-closed).

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.TransformationPipelineOptions
member.summary: Top-level configuration options for the request transformation pipeline. Bind these from appsettings.json under the Transformation key, or configure them programmatically via .

member.name: P:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.TransformationPipelineOptions.IsEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the transformation pipeline is active. Defaults to .

member.name: P:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.TransformationPipelineOptions.MaxRulesPerRequest
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of rules evaluated per request, across all scopes. Acts as a circuit-breaker against runaway rule sets. Defaults to 50.

member.name: P:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.TransformationPipelineOptions.StopOnError
member.summary: Gets or sets whether a non-fatal error in any single rule aborts the remainder of the pipeline () or logs the error and continues (). Defaults to so a bad rule does not break all transformations.

member.name: P:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.TransformationPipelineOptions.EnableBodyCapture
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the middleware buffers the request body so transformation rules and scripts can read and modify . Enabling body capture has a memory cost proportional to request size; consider pairing with a body size limit. Defaults to .

member.name: P:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.TransformationPipelineOptions.MaxBodySizeBytes
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum body size in bytes that will be buffered when is . Requests exceeding this limit are passed through without body capture. Defaults to 65 536 bytes (64 KiB).

member.name: P:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.TransformationPipelineOptions.RuleCacheTtl
member.summary: Gets or sets the time-to-live for the per-API-key rule cache maintained inside . Set to to disable caching entirely. Defaults to 60 s.

member.name: P:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.TransformationPipelineOptions.StaticRules
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of entries loaded directly from configuration (e.g. appsettings.json). This allows deploying simple rules without a database; for dynamic rule management use a database-backed implementation.

member.name: P:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.TransformationPipelineOptions.Lua
member.summary: Gets or sets the Lua script execution options.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.LuaExecutionOptions
member.summary: Configuration options governing Lua script execution inside the transformation pipeline.

member.name: P:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.LuaExecutionOptions.IsEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets whether Lua script execution is permitted. When , any rule is skipped rather than executed. Defaults to .

member.name: P:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.LuaExecutionOptions.MaxExecutionMs
member.summary: Gets or sets the wall-clock timeout in milliseconds applied per script invocation. Scripts that do not complete within this window are abandoned. Keep this low (≤ 50 ms) to prevent slow scripts from degrading gateway throughput. Defaults to 50 ms.

member.name: P:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.LuaExecutionOptions.MaxScriptSizeBytes
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum script source size in UTF-8 bytes. Scripts larger than this limit are rejected before execution begins. Defaults to 65 536 bytes (64 KiB).

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.ConfigurationTransformationRuleRepository
member.summary: Default backed by the static rule list in . Rules are held in memory and are read-only at runtime; mutations via , , and are not persisted across restarts. Replace this registration with a database-backed implementation for dynamic rule management.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Controllers.HealthController
member.summary: Health check endpoints for container orchestration and load balancers. These endpoints don't require API key authentication and help with: - Kubernetes liveness/readiness probes - Load balancer health checks - Container restart policies

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Controllers.HealthController.Live
member.summary: Liveness probe - indicates if the service is running. Used by Kubernetes to determine if pod should be restarted.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Controllers.HealthController.Ready
member.summary: Readiness probe - indicates if the service is ready to handle traffic. Includes checks for database connectivity and cache availability. Returns 503 with detailed error information when dependencies are not ready.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Controllers.HealthController.Status
member.summary: Detailed health status including component information. Returns 200 if all systems operational, 503 if degraded.

member.summary: Records a change between old and new values

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Domain.Models.AuditLogExtensions.ToSummaryString(ApiKeyGateway.Domain.Models.AuditLog)
member.summary: Generates a human-readable summary of the audit log entry.
member.param: The audit log entry to format.
member.returns: A formatted summary string containing timestamp, action description, resource information, performer, outcome, and HTTP status.
member.remarks: The format is: yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss: [action] on [ResourceType] [ResourceId] by [PerformedBy] - [outcome] ([HTTP status]) where outcome is either "Success" or "Failed: [error message]".

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Domain.Models.AuditLogExtensions.IsSecurityRelevant(ApiKeyGateway.Domain.Models.AuditLog)
member.summary: Determines whether the audit log entry represents a security-sensitive action. Security-relevant actions include unauthorized access attempts, key revocations, key disables, and rate limit violations.
member.param: The audit log entry to evaluate.
member.returns: if the action is security-related; otherwise .

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Domain.Models.AuditLogExtensions.GetMostRecentChange(ApiKeyGateway.Domain.Models.AuditLog)
member.summary: Retrieves the most recently recorded change from the audit log. The change is determined by the ChangedAt timestamp in the change value.
member.param: The audit log entry to process.
member.returns: An immutable dictionary containing the most recent change with keys Old, New, and ChangedAt, or an empty dictionary if no valid changes exist.
member.remarks: This method assumes that change values implement a structure with ChangedAt, Old, and New properties. Changes are ordered by ChangedAt in descending order to get the most recent.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Events.ApiKeyEvent
member.summary: Base class for all API key related events in the system. Events are fired when important state changes occur (key created, rotated, disabled). This allows different parts of the application to react to these changes without tight coupling - useful for audit logging, webhooks, metrics, etc.

member.summary: Fired when an API key is disabled or revoked.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Events.ApiKeyUpdatedEvent
member.summary: Fired when an API key's metadata (name, description) is updated.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Events.ApiKeyQuotaChangedEvent

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Events.AuditLogEventHandler
member.summary: Event handlers that react to domain events. These handlers implement cross-cutting concerns like audit logging, metrics collection, and webhook publishing triggered by key events. Decouples event producers from consumers for better maintainability.
member.remarks: Handlers are registered as singletons and subscribed once at startup, so they resolve scoped dependencies (repositories, tracking services) through per event instead of capturing them at construction time.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Middleware.ErrorHandlingMiddleware
member.summary: Centralized error handling middleware that catches all exceptions and returns standardized error responses. This prevents sensitive information from leaking to clients and ensures consistent error formatting across the API.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Middleware.PerformanceMonitoringMiddleware
member.summary: Middleware that monitors performance metrics and warns when endpoints exceed acceptable response times. This helps identify slow operations before they impact users. Thresholds are configurable per endpoint.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Middleware.RequestLoggingMiddleware
member.summary: Middleware that logs incoming HTTP requests and outgoing responses. This is critical for debugging, monitoring, and audit trail purposes. We measure response time to identify performance bottlenecks early.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Middleware.RequestTransformationMiddleware
member.summary: ASP.NET Core middleware that drives the request transformation pipeline. Positioned immediately after so it has access to the authenticated identity stored in context.Items.

remarks.para: The middleware builds a from the live , delegates execution to , and then writes any mutations (headers, query parameters, path, body) back onto the request before the pipeline continues to the next handler.
When any rule marks as the request is terminated with HTTP 403 Forbidden and the pipeline is short-circuited — no downstream middleware executes.
Body capture (controlled by ) enables scripts to read and rewrite the raw request body. Because body buffering incurs a memory allocation per request, it is disabled by default.

member.summary: Initialises a new instance of .

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Middleware.RequestTransformationMiddleware.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext)
member.summary: Processes the HTTP request through the transformation pipeline and either blocks the request (HTTP 403) or passes it to the next middleware with mutations applied.

member.summary: Adds the to the application request pipeline.
member.remarks: Must be called after UseApiKeyAuthentication() so that the authenticated identity is available to transformation rules.
member.returns: The original for fluent chaining.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Repositories.ApiKeyRepository
member.summary: Repository implementation for API key data persistence using ADO.NET with an in-memory write-through cache. All reads check the memory store first before querying the database, and all writes update both the database and cache atomically. Thread safety for concurrent access relies on the underlying implementation.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Services.IApiKeyRotationService
member.summary: Manages automated and manual API key rotation. Rotation creates a new key that inherits the old key's metadata and then revokes the old key so callers must upgrade to the new value.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Services.IApiKeyRotationService.RotateKeyAsync(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Rotates a single key by ID, creating a replacement and revoking the original.
member.param: The ID of the key to rotate.
Expiration for the new key in days. When is null the same TTL as the original key is used; if the original had no expiry the new key also has none.
member.returns: Result of the rotation operation.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Services.IApiKeyRotationService.RotateExpiringSoonAsync(System.Int32,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Rotates all active keys whose expiry falls within from now. Useful for proactive rotation of keys approaching expiration.
member.param: Expiration for the replacement keys. Defaults to the same TTL as each original.
member.returns: List of rotation results for all keys processed.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Services.IRequestCoalescingService
member.summary: Coalesces duplicate concurrent requests for the same logical resource into a single upstream operation, sharing its result across all waiting callers to reduce load on downstream services such as databases, caches, or external APIs.
member.remarks: When two or more concurrent callers invoke with the same requestKey, only the first ("leader") executes the provided operation. All subsequent callers ("followers") attach to the leader's in-flight task and receive the same result once the leader completes. If the leader faults or is cancelled, all followers observe the same exception or cancellation.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Services.IRequestCoalescingService.ExecuteAsync``1(System.String,System.Func{System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.Task{``0}},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Executes if no identical request is currently in flight; otherwise waits for the in-flight request to complete and returns its shared result.
member.typeparam: The result type produced by the operation.
member.param: A unique string key that identifies the logical request (e.g. a cache key, a hashed API key, or a resource identifier). Callers with the same key are coalesced.
A factory delegate that performs the actual work. Invoked only by the leading caller; followers share its result without invoking this delegate.
Token used to cancel a waiting follower. Cancellation of a follower does not abort the leader's operation; other followers continue to wait.
member.returns: The result produced by the leading operation.
member.exception: Thrown when is null or whitespace.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Services.IRequestCoalescingService.GetMetrics
member.summary: Returns a snapshot of coalescing metrics for observability and diagnostics.

member.summary: Thread-safe singleton implementation of backed by a and .

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Transformation.ITransformationPipeline
member.summary: Defines the contract for the request transformation pipeline. Implementations evaluate all matching entries in ascending priority order, applying header, query, path, body, and Lua-script transformations to an in-flight HTTP request before it reaches its destination handler.

member.summary: Applies every enabled rule that matches the current request context. Rules execute in ascending order; each rule observes mutations made by earlier rules. Processing halts early when any rule marks as .
member.param: The mutable transformation context wrapping the HTTP request. Callers should read mutations back from this object after the method returns.
Token used to abort long-running script execution.
member.returns: A summarising which rules ran, whether the request was blocked, and any non-fatal errors that occurred during rule execution.

The pipeline middleware writes final mutations back onto the live after all rules execute.

member.summary: Executes sandboxed Lua scripts within the request transformation pipeline. Each invocation receives an isolated MoonSharp environment populated with the current ; mutations made inside the script are reflected back into the context after execution completes.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Transformation.ILuaScriptExecutor.ExecuteAsync(System.String,ApiKeyGateway.Transformation.TransformationContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Executes the supplied Lua against the provided . The script receives a request global table with sub-tables for headers, query, and scalar fields (body, method, path, ip, consumer_id, api_key_id). All mutations to these tables are written back to on return.
member.param: Raw Lua source code to execute.
The transformation context exposed to the script. Mutations are reflected here.
Used to enforce the per-script wall-clock timeout configured in .
member.exception: Thrown when the Lua runtime raises an unhandled error during script execution.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Transformation.ILuaScriptExecutor.Validate(System.String)
member.summary: Performs static validation of Lua syntax and checks for access to disallowed globals without executing the script. Safe to call from an admin API endpoint before persisting a new rule.
member.param: Raw Lua source code to validate.
member.returns: A containing any syntax errors or security violations detected during static analysis.

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Transformation.LuaScriptExecutor
member.summary: MoonSharp-backed implementation of . Runs each script in an isolated instance with a stripped-down set of core modules to prevent file-system access, process execution, and network I/O.

remarks.para: The sandbox exposes only Basic, String, Table, Math, and Bit32 module sets. OS, IO, debug, and dynamic-load facilities are excluded.

Script execution is delegated to a worker thread so the calling async context is not blocked. A linked enforces the wall-clock timeout; if the script exceeds the limit the task is abandoned and a timeout error is recorded — note that a tight CPU-bound loop inside Lua cannot be pre-empted by cancellation alone, so script authors should keep operations O(1) or O(n) with small n.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Transformation.LuaScriptExecutor.#ctor(ApiKeyGateway.Configuration.LuaExecutionOptions,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{ApiKeyGateway.Transformation.LuaScriptExecutor})
member.summary: Initialises a new instance of .

member.name: T:ApiKeyGateway.Transformation.TransformationPipelineService
member.summary: Core engine that orchestrates the request transformation pipeline. Resolves all entries that match the incoming request, executes them in ascending priority order, and applies mutations (header injection, query rewriting, path rewriting, Lua scripts) to the mutable before the request proceeds to its handler.

remarks.para: Rule sets are loaded concurrently from global, per-consumer, and per-key scopes, then merged and deduplicated before execution begins.
A lightweight rule-cache keyed on API key identifier reduces repository round-trips on hot paths. The cache entry expires after .

member.summary: Initialises a new instance of .

member.summary: Validates a Lua script without executing it. Performs static syntax and security checks. Safe to call from an admin API endpoint before persisting a new rule.
member.param: Raw Lua source code to validate.
member.returns: A containing any errors detected.

member.summary: Cryptography and hashing utility methods

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Utilities.CryptoHelpers.GenerateSecureRandomString(System.Int32)
member.summary: Generates a cryptographically secure random string. Delegates to , which performs unbiased (rejection-sampled) selection; a plain modulo over the RNG output would skew the distribution because 2^32 is not divisible by the alphabet size.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Utilities.CryptoHelpers.ComputeSha256Hash(System.String)
member.summary: Computes SHA-256 hash of a string. Uses ArrayPool for the UTF-8 encoding buffer and stackalloc for the 32-byte hash output, keeping GC pressure near zero on the hot path.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Utilities.CryptoHelpers.VerifyHash(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Verifies a hash against an input value using a fixed-time comparison so the check does not leak how many leading characters matched.

member.name: M:ApiKeyGateway.Utilities.CryptoHelpers.ComputeHmacSha256(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Computes HMAC-SHA256 signature. Uses ArrayPool for both key and message encoding buffers and the static HMACSHA256.HashData overload to avoid allocating an HMACSHA256 instance.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.CreateDocumentationId(System.Reflection.MethodInfo)
member.summary: Generates a documentation comment ID for a method (or constructor). For example: M:Namespace.ContainingType.MethodName(ParamType1,ParamType2)~ReturnType M:Namespace.ContainingType.#ctor(ParamType)

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.GetTypeDocId(System.Type,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Generates a documentation ID string for a type. This method handles nested types (replacing '+' with '.'), generic types, arrays, pointers, by-ref types, and generic parameters. The flag controls whether constructed generic type arguments are emitted, while controls whether the generic arity marker (e.g. "`1") is appended.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.NormalizeDocId(System.String)
member.summary: Normalizes a documentation comment ID to match the compiler-style format. Strips the return type suffix for ordinary methods but retains it for conversion operators.
member.param: The documentation comment ID to normalize.
member.returns: The normalized documentation comment ID.
